body:
## PR is ready to merge

Only failed checks and required actions are listed below.

All CI checks have passed (checkenforcer: âś… All checks passed). No pipeline failures detected.

<!-- mgmt-review-assistant -->

đź”´ **Tool Issue** â€” `CHANGELOG.md:46`

The breaking change `- Operation Services.checkNameAvailability has a new signature` was removed from this changelog entry by the regeneration tool, but the API **did** change in this PR: the `checkNameAvailability` function signature changed from accepting a `CheckNameAvailabilityInput` object to accepting `name: string` directly.

The base branch (`main`) already had this entry from a prior generation, but this new regeneration removed it incorrectly.

**Fix:** Add the missing entry back to the `Breaking Changes` section:
```
  - Operation Services.checkNameAvailability has a new signature
```
Report this in the [generation tool repository](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues).

đź”´ **Tool Issue** â€” `review/arm-search-api-sharedPrivateLinkResources-node.api.md:28`

The `clientRequestId?: string` property was **removed** from both `SharedPrivateLinkResourcesCreateOrUpdateOptionalParams` (this line) and `SharedPrivateLinkResourcesDeleteOptionalParams` (line 35), replacing it with `searchManagementRequestOptions?: SearchManagementRequestOptions`.

This is a **breaking change** â€” callers who were passing `clientRequestId` directly in the options object will have a compilation error. However, the `CHANGELOG.md` `Breaking Changes` section does not mention this removal.

**Fix:** Add the following to the `Breaking Changes` section in `CHANGELOG.md`:
```
  - Interface SharedPrivateLinkResourcesCreateOrUpdateOptionalParams no longer has parameter clientRequestId
  - Interface SharedPrivateLinkResourcesDeleteOptionalParams no longer has parameter clientRequestId
```
The property is now accessible via `searchManagementRequestOptions?.clientRequestId`. Report this in the [generation tool repository](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues).

2 tool issues found in the generated `CHANGELOG.md` for `@azure/arm-search@4.0.0-beta.2`.

